title: Stop, Look, and Hold Hands
lesson: Stop, look both ways, and hold a grown-up's hand to cross the road safely.
prompts: On a city street with a pedestrian crossing, clear daylight, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image walks along the pavement, with a cheerful expression. Nearby, {GENDER_M_AGE_35_PARENT} walks beside them. Make the interaction read physically: each person faces the other person or the shared object, with hands and gaze directed to one connected action. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

On a city street with a pedestrian crossing, clear daylight, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image is at the edge of the kerb, leaning forward as if about to step toward the road, with a surprised expression. Nearby, {GENDER_M_AGE_35_PARENT} gently holds them back with a caring, watchful look. A parked car waits nearby. Make the interaction read physically: each person faces the other person or the shared object, with hands and gaze directed to one connected action. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

On a city street with a pedestrian crossing, clear daylight, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands at the kerb, with an attentive expression, turning their head to look both ways along the road. Nearby, {GENDER_M_AGE_35_PARENT} points left and right, teaching them to look. Make the interaction read physically: each person faces the other person or the shared object, with hands and gaze directed to one connected action. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

On a city street with a pedestrian crossing, clear daylight, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands at the crossing, holding the grown-up's hand and waiting beside a traffic light showing a red figure, with a patient expression. Nearby, {GENDER_M_AGE_35_PARENT} waits calmly, holding their hand. Make the interaction read physically: each person faces the other person or the shared object, with hands and gaze directed to one connected action. Eye-level medium shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

On a city street with a pedestrian crossing, clear daylight, the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image walks across the white zebra crossing, with a confident smile, holding hands with {GENDER_M_AGE_35_PARENT} who walks beside them. A green walking signal glows ahead. Make the interaction read physically: each person faces the other person or the shared object, with hands and gaze directed to one connected action. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.

On a city street with a pedestrian crossing, clear daylight, safely on the far pavement the {INPUT_1_AGE} person from the input image stands, giving a happy thumbs-up, with a proud smile. Nearby, {GENDER_M_AGE_35_PARENT} gives a thumbs-up and a warm smile. Make the interaction read physically: each person faces the other person or the shared object, with hands and gaze directed to one connected action. Eye-level medium-wide shot, photorealistic, cinematic photography style. Preserve the facial features, skin tone and hairstyle of the person from the input image.
gists: On a city street with a pedestrian crossing in daylight, the child walks along the pavement, cheerful, while the father walks beside them.
At the kerb, the child leans forward as if about to step into the road with a surprised look while the father gently holds them back, caring and watchful, a parked car nearby — stop first.
At the kerb, the child turns their head to look both ways along the road, attentive, while the father points left and right, teaching them to look.
At the crossing by a traffic light showing red, the child waits patiently holding the father's hand while the father waits calmly holding their hand — waiting for the signal.
The child walks across the white zebra crossing, confident, holding hands with the father, a green walking signal glowing ahead — crossing safely together.
Safely on the far pavement, the child gives a happy thumbs-up, proud, while the father gives a thumbs-up and a warm smile — crossed the road safely.
texts: {NAME} walked along the busy pavement with Dad. Up ahead was the road. "Stay close to me," said Dad. "Okay!" said {NAME}.
{NAME} started to step toward the road, but Dad gently held {NAME} back. "Wait — we must always stop first," he said. "Oops!" said {NAME}.
"Now look left, and look right," said Dad. {NAME} turned and looked both ways along the road. "All clear?" asked {NAME}. "Not yet," said Dad.
The light showed red, so they waited patiently, holding hands. "When can we go?" asked {NAME}. "When it turns green," said Dad. "We wait."
The light turned green. "Now we can go," said Dad. Holding hands, {NAME} and Dad walked safely across the stripes. Step, step, step!
Safe on the other side! Dad gave a big thumbs-up. "Well done!" he said. "Stop, look, and hold hands!" cheered {NAME}.
